summary: Verified the 3 deltas of the production-grade Forgot Password flow: (a) special-char + whitespace + max-length strength rules, (b) confirmation email + audit metadata, (c) live 6-rule checklist + field-level mismatch/match UX. 14/14 backend pytest cases PASSED. All 11 frontend Playwright assertions PASSED (rule rows, real-time data-ok flips, mismatch/match indicators, submit disabled/enabled gating, in-flight 'Updating…' state, success block, auto-redirect to /login, no auto-login, mobile 375px no horizontal scroll, invalid token UI). Audit log confirmed to contain confirmation_email_sent + confirmation_email_error fields on success and reason=weak_password on failure. Password successfully restored to TestPass123! at end of run.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: _password_is_strong message ordering (lowercase before uppercase before digit before special before space) gives users a deterministic next-step in the live UX.

GOOD: Same-password reuse blocked (verify_password check before consume). Important: reuse path emits audit reason='same_as_current' BEFORE consume — token remains valid, which is the correct behavior so the user can retry with a different password.
GOOD: Confirmation email send wrapped in try/except so a Resend outage cannot break the reset. confirmation_email_sent + confirmation_email_error both recorded for support correlation.
GOOD: Frontend re-derives ruleStatus via useMemo on every keystroke; data-ok='0'/'1' attribute makes the live checklist trivially testable from outside the React tree.

MINOR: ResetPasswordRequest Pydantic model uses min_length=1 for new_password/confirm_password — so a 1-char payload returns 400 weak_password (good) but it's worth surfacing min_length=8 at the schema layer too for a faster validation hop (currently still works correctly via _password_is_strong).
test_credentials: sr-tester@example.com / TestPass123! (restored to original at end of run; verified with a live /api/auth/login → 200)
seed_data_creation: Test mints fresh reset tokens directly into db.password_reset_tokens for each test that needs one (no production seed mutated). auth_rate_limits is wiped session-start via autouse fixture to keep tests deterministic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression file at /app/backend/tests/test_password_reset_v2.py — run with `pytest backend/tests/test_password_reset_v2.py -v`. Tests dynamically mint fresh tokens via Motor against MONGO_URL+DB_NAME from /app/backend/.env (strips surrounding quotes). pytest-asyncio is now installed but the suite is written sync-with-asyncio.run helpers so it does not depend on the plugin's mode. Auth rate limits are wiped at session start. If you need to re-test the forgot flow, sr-tester@example.com/TestPass123! is the canonical account; tests restore the password at the end so they are repeatable.

deltas_verified.strength_rules: Special-char required → 400 weak_password with 'special character' in message; whitespace rejected → 400 weak_password with 'space' in message; >200 chars rejected (400/422).
deltas_verified.confirmation_email_and_audit: db.login_events.password_reset_completed.metadata contains confirmation_email_sent (bool), confirmation_email_error (str|null), sessions_invalidated (int ≥1 in our test). Failed weak attempts produce password_reset_failed with reason='weak_password'.
deltas_verified.live_checklist_ux: All 6 reset-rule-{len,upper,lower,digit,special,nospace} render with data-ok='0' initially, flip to data-ok='1' as each criterion is met in real time. reset-confirm-mismatch and reset-confirm-match toggle correctly. reset-submit-btn disabled until allRulesPass && matches && !loading. In-flight text becomes 'Updating…' and button is disabled. After 200, reset-success shows and page auto-redirects to /login within 2.5s with no session_token stored — user MUST sign in manually.
